name: Apple
description: Premium product reference — pure white surface, true black anchor, SF Pro display at scale. Focal product photography carries the narrative; type and color stay disciplined. Restraint is the brand.

--color-paper-base.role: Primary light surface — page background, product hero base

--color-jet-anchor.role: True black sections — pro-product reveals, dark mode pages
--color-jet-anchor.context: absolute black, used for theatrical contrast

--color-graphite-body.role: Primary text on white — near-black with the slightest warmth
--color-graphite-body.context: Apple signature off-black, easier on the eye than #000

--color-slate-muted.context: neutral mid-grey, calibrated for legal copy and tertiary info

--color-azure-link.role: Inline links and 'Learn more' CTAs only — never used as surface fill

--color-cloud-divider.context: warm light grey, near-invisible at scale

--color-shadow-surface.role: Alternating section background — gallery cards, secondary surfaces
--color-shadow-surface.context: barely-warmer-than-white, gives sections breath without weight

--font-display-sf.role: Display headlines — product names at hero scale, eyebrows

antiPatterns: Never use --color-azure-link as a button fill — Apple reserves it strictly for inline text affordances. Buttons are black, white, or transparent with a stroke.
Never combine more than 2 colors per section — Apple sections are monochromatic except for product imagery. A page with 4+ accent colors stops looking like Apple.
Body text must never exceed 21px — once body type passes 24px it stops reading as Apple and starts looking like a startup pitch deck.
Never set --color-graphite-body on --color-jet-anchor — Apple dark sections use pure white text or pure-grey scales. Off-black on black is muddy.
